items.title: Subagents have arrived in Gemini CLI
items.summary: Google added subagent support to Gemini CLI so developers can delegate narrower tasks inside the command-line workflow instead of relying on one long monolithic session.

items.title: Changes to GitHub Copilot plans for individuals
items.summary: GitHub detailed plan changes for individual Copilot users, updating how model access, premium capabilities, and usage are packaged across paid tiers.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: pricing and packaging shifts often reveal where real demand and cost pressure are landing in AI coding.

items.title: Developer's guide to building ADK agents with skills
items.summary: Google published a practical guide for wiring reusable skills into ADK agents, showing how teams can structure, compose, and extend agent behaviors more systematically.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: agent builders increasingly differentiate on reusable skills and orchestration patterns, not just on model choice.

items.title: Copilot in Visual Studio: March update
items.summary: GitHub rolled new Copilot improvements into Visual Studio, adding model and chat workflow updates that keep more coding assistance inside the IDE.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: IDE-native Copilot updates show how the coding-agent race is expanding beyond the browser and terminal into incumbent desktop workflows.

items.title: Partnering with Mozilla to improve Firefox's security
items.summary: Anthropic said it is working with Mozilla to strengthen Firefox security workflows with Claude-backed assistance for threat analysis and defensive review.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: frontier model vendors are pushing deeper into real security operations where trust and guardrails matter as much as raw capability.

items.title: Evaluating the ethics of autonomous systems
items.summary: MIT researchers outlined a framework for evaluating autonomous systems through an ethics lens, focusing on how AI systems are assessed before higher-stakes deployment.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: governance pressure is rising alongside capability, so evaluation frameworks are becoming part of the product stack.

items.title: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
items.summary: OpenAI said Hyatt is extending ChatGPT Enterprise across more employee workflows to speed up research, writing, and operational knowledge sharing.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise AI adoption is increasingly measured by organization-wide usage, not just isolated pilot teams.

items.title: Infor and AWS bring agentic AI to manufacturing at enterprise scale
items.summary: Infor and AWS said they are bringing agentic AI into manufacturing software flows so teams can connect enterprise data, automation, and operations decisions more directly.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise AI is shifting from standalone copilots toward agents embedded in line-of-business systems.

items.title: Helping data centers deliver higher performance with less hardware
items.summary: MIT researchers described a method for improving data-center performance with less hardware, aiming to cut system overhead while keeping AI infrastructure efficient at scale.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI infrastructure gains increasingly come from efficiency work that squeezes more throughput from existing compute.

items.title: AWS collaborates with SHI India to enable AI infrastructure for IndiaAI Mission
items.summary: AWS and SHI India said they will support IndiaAI Mission infrastructure with cloud and AI stack capacity aimed at expanding national access to model training and deployment resources.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: compute availability remains a bottleneck, so infrastructure deals are becoming strategic AI news in their own right.

items.title: Enterprises power agentic workflows in Cloudflare Agent Cloud with OpenAI
items.summary: OpenAI said Cloudflare is bringing frontier OpenAI models into Agent Cloud so enterprises can deploy GPT-5.4 and Codex-powered agents inside production-ready edge and sandbox workflows.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise agent adoption is shifting from demos into hosted infrastructure where deployment, governance, and latency matter as much as model access.

items.title: Gemini API gets a tooling update with richer agent workflow controls
items.summary: Google added context circulation, mixed tool calling, and Google Maps grounding for Gemini so developers can build more capable agent workflows without stitching together as much orchestration logic by hand.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: agent builders increasingly compete on workflow plumbing and tool orchestration, not only on raw model quality.

items.title: Remote control CLI sessions on web and mobile enter public preview
items.summary: GitHub introduced remote control for Copilot CLI sessions so developers can monitor, steer, and continue an active coding-agent session from github.com or the GitHub mobile apps.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: coding agents are becoming persistent, cross-device workflows rather than tools that only live inside one local terminal window.

items.title: GitHub lets Copilot cloud agent fix merge conflicts in three clicks
items.summary: GitHub added a new Fix with Copilot flow on github.com so Copilot cloud agent can resolve merge conflicts, validate the branch, and push the updated result from its own cloud workspace.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: software teams increasingly want coding agents to own annoying workflow repair work, not just code generation.

items.title: Trusted access for the next era of cyber defense
items.summary: OpenAI detailed how it is scaling Trusted Access for Cyber, including expanded defender verification and a more cyber-permissive GPT-5.4-Cyber path for higher-trust use cases.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: advanced cyber models are being rolled out through increasingly granular access tiers instead of one-size-fits-all availability.

items.title: OpenAI expands its cyber defense ecosystem with more defenders and grants
items.summary: OpenAI expanded its Trusted Access for Cyber efforts with new enterprise security participants, broader grant support, and more pathways for defenders working on critical digital infrastructure.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: frontier AI deployment is increasingly tied to who can safely use more capable models and how fast those safeguards can scale with demand.

items.title: A better method for identifying overconfident large language models
items.summary: MIT researchers introduced a method for spotting when language models are overconfident, aiming to give developers a better way to judge uncertainty before those systems are trusted in higher-stakes settings.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: model adoption increasingly depends on better uncertainty signals, not just better benchmark scores.

items.title: Anthropic launches Project Glasswing to secure critical software with frontier AI
items.summary: Anthropic launched Project Glasswing to give critical software defenders early access to Claude Mythos Preview and broader support for vulnerability discovery and remediation work.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: frontier labs are using gated deployments and industry coalitions to push advanced cybersecurity capability toward defense before offense catches up.

items.title: OpenAI introduces the Child Safety Blueprint
items.summary: OpenAI published a new child-safety framework focused on AI-enabled exploitation risks, combining policy proposals, reporting improvements, and safety-by-design measures.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI providers are pairing model launches with more explicit policy and enforcement frameworks for high-risk misuse domains.

items.title: AI system learns to keep warehouse robot traffic running smoothly
items.summary: MIT researchers built a learning system that helps warehouse robots avoid traffic jams and keep more autonomous machines moving efficiently through busy fulfillment environments.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: practical AI gains often come from making robotics systems more reliable inside real operational bottlenecks rather than from headline model launches alone.

items.title: AWS and OpenAI announce multi-year strategic partnership
items.summary: AWS and OpenAI announced a multi-year partnership that will put advanced OpenAI workloads on AWS infrastructure as the companies deepen their cloud relationship.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: infrastructure alliances are becoming a strategic lever for model labs trying to secure compute and distribution at scale.

items.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-generation compute
items.summary: Anthropic said it is expanding work with Google and Broadcom to secure multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute for future model development and deployment.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: compute access remains one of the clearest strategic bottlenecks in the frontier model race.

items.title: Anthropic invests $100 million into the Claude Partner Network
items.summary: Anthropic launched the Claude Partner Network with a $100 million commitment to help partner organizations support enterprise adoption of Claude.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: frontier model vendors are scaling through service ecosystems and implementation partners, not just direct product sales.

items.title: GitHub broadens Copilot cloud agent from PR tasks to research and planning work
items.summary: GitHub expanded Copilot cloud agent beyond pull-request workflows so it can take on more research, planning, and coding work before changes ever reach review.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: coding assistants are shifting from inline helpers to longer-horizon agents that can own more of the software loop.

items.title: Prepay for the Gemini API to get more control over your spend
items.summary: Google introduced prepaid Gemini API credits so developers can manage budgets more tightly and get clearer control over AI infrastructure spend before usage accrues.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: as model costs become a line-item decision, pricing controls and billing flexibility are becoming product features in their own right.

items.title: Introducing Learn Mode: your personal coding tutor in Google Colab
items.summary: Google launched Learn Mode in Colab to guide users through code and notebook workflows with more tutor-like step-by-step support instead of only returning direct answers.

items.title: Turn your best AI prompts into one-click tools in Chrome
items.summary: Google added a new Chrome workflow that turns saved prompts into reusable one-click tools so users can trigger repeatable AI actions without rebuilding prompts each time.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: browser-native AI is shifting from one-off prompting toward lightweight workflow automation that users can keep close to where work already happens.

items.title: Copilot SDK enters public preview for app and workflow builders
items.summary: GitHub opened the Copilot SDK in public preview so teams can embed Copilot's agentic capabilities more directly into their own apps, workflows, and platform services.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: agent platforms are moving from standalone products into infrastructure that other software teams can wire into their own surfaces.

items.title: GitHub makes organization-wide Copilot custom instructions generally available
items.summary: GitHub made organization custom instructions generally available for Copilot Business and Enterprise so admins can steer default assistant behavior across teams.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise AI rollouts increasingly depend on shared defaults and governance controls, not just individual user prompts.

items.title: GitHub adds cloud-agent activity into Copilot usage metrics
items.summary: GitHub updated Copilot usage metrics so cloud-agent active user counts roll into reporting, giving teams a clearer read on how agent workflows are actually being adopted.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: once agent deployments grow, measurement and accountability become as important as model capability.

items.title: Q&A: MIT SHASS and the future of education in the age of AI
items.summary: MIT's School of Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences argued that human understanding and broad education matter even more as AI reshapes how students learn and work.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the education debate around AI is moving from novelty tools to the long-term shape of how people think, learn, and reason.

items.title: Introducing The Anthropic Institute
items.summary: Anthropic launched The Anthropic Institute as a new effort focused on preparing for the broader societal challenges created by more powerful AI systems.

items.title: Generate 3D models and interactive charts with the Gemini app
items.summary: Google expanded the Gemini app so users can create 3D models and interactive charts directly from prompts, pushing the product further into visual and data-rich creation tasks.

items.title: New technique makes AI models leaner and faster while they are still learning
items.summary: MIT researchers introduced CompreSSM, a method that trims model complexity during training so AI systems can keep learning while becoming leaner and more efficient.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: efficiency work is becoming a core frontier in AI because it cuts compute cost without waiting for the next giant model release.

items.title: ModMed selects AWS as the cloud provider for the AI-powered practice
items.summary: Amazon said ModMed is standardizing on AWS to support an AI-powered practice stack built for specialty medicine workflows and clinical software operations.

items.summary: GitHub rolled out Claude Opus 4.7 as a generally available model option in Copilot, expanding model choice for developer teams.

items.summary: GitHub added custom-property controls that let enterprises selectively enable Copilot cloud agent access for specific organizations.

items.summary: Canva upgraded its AI assistant so it can invoke multiple tools across the product to help users build finished designs with less manual work.

items.summary: Amazon introduced Bio Discovery, a service designed to help researchers use AI agents and large-scale computation across drug-discovery workflows.

items.summary: OpenAI is arguing that AI can accelerate biomedical research by connecting fragmented knowledge, designing tools, automating labs, and shortening experiment cycles.

items.title: Claude outage hits Claude.ai, API, and Claude Code users
items.summary: Anthropic confirmed elevated errors affecting Claude.ai, the API, and Claude Code, with users reporting login failures and interrupted prompt handling during the April 15 incident.

items.title: Gitar emerges with $9M for agentic code security
items.summary: Gitar emerged from stealth with $9 million for an agent-based platform aimed at helping teams secure code.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI coding tools are creating demand for automated review and remediation systems that can keep up with faster software generation.

items.title: Emergent moves into vibe-coding AI agents
items.summary: India-based Emergent is expanding from vibe coding into an OpenClaw-like AI agent space for building and operating software workflows.

items.title: Parasail raises $32M for tokenmaxxing AI developers
items.summary: Parasail raised $32 million for infrastructure aimed at developers who need to spend and manage large token volumes for AI products.

items.summary: Gizmo, an AI learning app focused on study workflows, raised $22 million after growing to 13 million users.

items.title: Hightouch hits $100M ARR on AI-powered marketing tools
items.summary: Hightouch says its AI marketing product helped drive the company to $100 million in ARR by letting brands generate personalized campaigns from their own creative systems.

items.title: OpenAI's cyber model rollout tests the market for verified AI defense access
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.4-Cyber launch gives vetted security vendors, organizations, and researchers access to stronger defensive tooling while keeping the program gated.

items.title: Anthropic Mythos forces a harder government conversation about AI cyber defense
items.summary: Anthropic's Mythos Preview has renewed debate over how public agencies should use frontier AI for critical-infrastructure defense amid CISA staffing and funding pressure.

items.title: Microsoft tests OpenClaw-like agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot

items.title: Nvidia launches open Ising AI models for quantum computing
items.summary: Nvidia introduced the Ising open model family to help researchers and enterprises improve quantum processor calibration and error correction.

items.summary: Synera raised a $40 million Series B to scale an agentic AI platform that orchestrates engineering workflows across existing design and simulation tools.

items.title: Ranger AI surfaces as another industrial agent workflow bet
items.summary: Ranger AI, an AI agent platform for industrial RFP response and tendering workflows, reported a $6 million funding round in the April 14 startup funding report.

items.summary: Bluefish raised $43 million in Series B funding for a platform that helps brands understand and influence how they appear inside AI-generated discovery surfaces.

items.title: GitHub adds model selection for Claude and Codex agents
items.summary: GitHub now lets users choose models when starting Claude and Codex third-party coding agent tasks on github.com, including Claude Sonnet and Opus options plus GPT-5.2-Codex, GPT-5.3-Codex, and GPT-5.4.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: agent platforms are turning model choice into a first-class workflow control instead of hiding it behind a single assistant brand.

items.title: Human-machine teaming dives underwater

items.summary: Cursor's latest release makes multi-agent coding sessions easier to supervise by adding a tiled layout in the Agents Window and upgrading dictation with batch speech-to-text.

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live becomes Google's clearest push into always-on voice AI
items.summary: Google is pushing Gemini deeper into real-time assistant workflows with a lower-latency audio model that feels built for live conversation, demos, and multimodal agent experiences.

items.title: Perplexity keeps using Computer to reposition itself as a builder platform
items.summary: Perplexity is using a public startup challenge to frame Computer as more than a research assistant, pushing it toward an agent platform with broader builder appeal.

items.title: ChatGPT's new $100 plan makes Codex-heavy usage feel more mainstream
items.summary: OpenAI's new pricing layer widens the on-ramp for heavier ChatGPT and Codex usage, making advanced agent workflows feel less niche than a single premium paywall approach.
items.excerpt: Heat read: with ChatGPT still one of the highest-traffic AI products online, pricing moves are effectively market signals about where real demand is concentrating.

items.title: Adobe Firefly shifts from wow-factor generation toward exact image control
items.summary: Adobe's latest Firefly release is less about one-click novelty and more about precise editing controls that fit real creative-team revision loops inside an existing production stack.

items.title: Claude Managed Agents gives Anthropic a more complete hosted agent stack
items.summary: Anthropic is turning Claude from a strong chat surface into more durable agent infrastructure, with hosted sessions designed for longer-running, recoverable production work.
items.excerpt: Heat read: Claude remains one of the highest-traffic top tools on the site, so managed orchestration is a more meaningful signal than another generic model benchmark.

items.title: Gemma 4 strengthens Google's open-weight story for AI Studio builders

items.title: DeepSeek V3-0324 keeps raising pressure on proprietary model leaders
items.summary: DeepSeek continues to gain attention as one of the few open-model challengers forcing Gemini, Claude, and ChatGPT to respond to faster-moving price and capability pressure.
items.excerpt: Tool angle: DeepSeek remains one of the clearest open-model stories inside this site's top-traffic AI stack.

items.title: Codex at 3M weekly users shows coding agents are crossing into the mainstream
items.summary: The latest Codex growth milestone suggests AI coding agents are no longer just for early adopters and power users, but are becoming part of the default developer toolkit.
items.excerpt: Tool angle: ChatGPT and Codex are increasingly winning not just on model quality but on repeated, habitual use.

items.title: Claude Mythos keeps pushing Anthropic toward a cyber-and-safety-first release posture
items.summary: Anthropic's Mythos messaging is positioning Claude as a higher-stakes model family, with benchmark gains wrapped in a more defensive release narrative around security risk.
items.excerpt: Tool angle: this is less about a single launch and more about how Anthropic wants the market to interpret Claude's next frontier step.

items.title: Google adds stronger mental-health safeguards to Gemini after lawsuits
items.summary: Google is widening Gemini's safety posture with crisis hotlines and anti-self-harm design changes, showing how trust and liability are becoming product-level concerns.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the next competitive layer is not just capability, but whether products can operate safely at consumer scale.

items.title: Notebooks in Gemini brings longer-lived project context into Google's consumer stack
items.summary: Google's new notebooks layer makes Gemini feel more useful for recurring research and presentation work by turning one-off chats into reusable, project-based context.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: persistent context is one of the most practical product levers for keeping users inside one AI ecosystem.

items.title: Adobe pushes Firefly-adjacent study workflows into Acrobat with Student Spaces
items.summary: Adobe is extending its AI footprint beyond image generation by turning Acrobat into a study and synthesis tool for notes, flashcards, podcasts, and presentations.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Adobe is using existing workflow surfaces, not standalone hype, to deepen AI usage.

items.title: DeepL is positioning AI Agent as a multilingual workflow layer for business teams
items.summary: DeepL is framing AI Agent as more than translation, pushing toward a broader business assistant layer for international teams handling support, writing, and operations.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: DeepL remains one of the most commercially credible AI tools outside the big model labs.

items.title: OpenAI's Illinois liability push shows regulation is now a product-adjacent battle
items.summary: OpenAI's support for an Illinois liability bill highlights how legal definitions around AI responsibility are becoming a meaningful competitive issue for major labs.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: regulation is no longer outside the product story; it directly shapes deployment risk.

items.title: OpenAI's $122B funding round reset expectations for frontier-AI scale
items.summary: OpenAI's enormous raise reinforces that ChatGPT's commercial position is now influencing how the entire market thinks about compute, infrastructure, and capital requirements.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: funding scale now shapes which labs can keep pushing expensive frontier products.

items.title: Grok expands inside X with translation and photo-editing features
items.summary: X is widening Grok's usefulness with built-in translation and image-editing tools, reinforcing its strategy of making the assistant feel native to the platform rather than separate from it.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Grok's scale advantage comes from distribution through X, not just model branding.

items.title: Claude Cowork reaches general availability on desktop for enterprise teams
items.summary: Anthropic moved Claude Cowork to general availability on macOS and Windows, adding usage analytics, OpenTelemetry support, and tighter enterprise controls around rollout.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Anthropic is turning Claude from a strong assistant into a more governable workplace product with adoption tracking and admin controls.

items.title: Claude Code's leak turned Anthropic's harness into a public design reference
items.summary: The Claude Code source leak made the orchestration layer behind one of the hottest coding agents unusually legible, giving the market a clearer look at how serious agent harnesses are being built.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the AI coding race increasingly looks like a harness race, not just a model race.

items.title: Perplexity Computer keeps stretching from research into end-to-end task execution
items.summary: Perplexity's push into tasks like tax prep shows the company wants Computer to be seen as an action-taking agent, not just a better search box.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the market is rewarding products that can actually complete work, not only summarize it.

items.title: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google align more openly against model-copying pressure
items.summary: The leading labs are taking a harder public line on distillation and model copying, a sign that open-model pressure from players like DeepSeek is reshaping the rules of competition.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: open-model competition is now triggering strategy changes inside the biggest closed-model labs.

items.title: Google AI Studio gets a stronger developer story when Gemma and Gemini move together
items.summary: Gemma 4 gives Google AI Studio more open-model credibility, making Google's stack feel broader than a single closed Gemini endpoint for developers and prototype teams.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the most durable platforms usually win by offering multiple lanes, not one monolithic model.

items.title: Project Glasswing gives defenders early access to Anthropic's unreleased cyber model
items.summary: Anthropic is using Project Glasswing to put Mythos Preview in defenders' hands first, signaling a more security-first release strategy for high-risk Claude capabilities.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Claude's strongest upcoming capabilities are being framed as a deployment-governance problem, not just a product launch.

items.title: DeepL is broadening from translation into agent-style enterprise assistance
items.summary: DeepL's AI Agent positioning suggests the company wants to own more of the multilingual workflow stack, not just the final translation step.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: specialized AI products can still grow by expanding around one trusted core job.

items.title: GitHub Copilot expands agent control and browser debugging in VS Code
items.summary: GitHub's March releases for Copilot in VS Code added Autopilot sessions, finer agent permissions, integrated browser debugging, and richer media support inside chat.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: GitHub is pushing Copilot further into full-session autonomous development instead of keeping it as a narrow inline completion tool.

items.title: Meta launches Muse Spark to upgrade Meta AI across apps and API preview
items.summary: Meta introduced Muse Spark as the first model from Meta Superintelligence Labs, using it to power faster multimodal Meta AI experiences and a private preview API for partners.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Meta is tying a new flagship model directly to consumer distribution across WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and AI glasses.

items.title: Anthropic's huge TPU deal shows Claude's growth is now gated by infrastructure scale
items.summary: Anthropic's expanded compute arrangement with Google and Broadcom makes clear that serving and training Claude at the frontier is now as much an infrastructure story as a model story.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: demand for top assistants is increasingly constrained by how much compute can actually be delivered.

items.title: OpenAI's policy paper reads like a mini-New Deal for the AI economy
items.summary: OpenAI is no longer speaking only as a lab shipping ChatGPT upgrades, but as a company actively trying to shape how governments respond to large-scale AI disruption.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: AI policy is becoming a direct extension of platform strategy.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic are being discussed more like future IPO companies than labs
items.summary: Reporting around IPO pressure shows how quickly the frontier-model race is being financialized as ChatGPT and Claude keep scaling revenue and compute spend at the same time.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the market is increasingly evaluating AI leaders as giant infrastructure businesses, not just products.

items.title: Perplexity's reported ARR momentum suggests research products can still break out
items.summary: Reported revenue momentum is reinforcing Perplexity's shift from interesting search startup to one of the few serious consumer AI products with clear commercial pull.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: revenue traction matters because it shows users are paying for a research-native alternative to the biggest general assistants.

items.title: Figma opens its canvas to AI agents with an MCP server beta
items.summary: Figma now lets AI agents create and edit design assets on the canvas through its MCP server beta, with skills used to encode design-system rules and workflow context.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: design tools are becoming part of the agent loop, not just a handoff destination after code is written.

items.title: ChatGPT adds a File Library for saved uploads and generated assets
items.summary: OpenAI added a persistent Library in ChatGPT so uploaded files and generated assets can be reused later, with recent-file insertion and sidebar browsing across web and mobile surfaces.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: persistent files make ChatGPT feel more like an ongoing workspace than a disposable chat window.
